uses: Mariscus affinis has been traditionally used in various folk remedies due to its purported medicinal properties. Its leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. intro: Mariscus affinis, also known as East Indian sedge, is a type of plant in the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Mariscus affinis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and dried powder, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
